title: potentilla recta
intro: Potentilla recta, also known as the sulfur cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species in the rose family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat various skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. The herbal preparations of Potentilla recta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, applied topically as creams or ointments, or taken as capsules or tablets.
